description: JS/TS Monorepo Detection - Extract workspace package names from package.json/pnpm-workspace.yaml so internal npm/yarn/pnpm workspace imports (e.g. @myorg/shared) are recognised by the dependency resolver instead of being dropped as external.

userStories.title: Add js_monorepo test fixture
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a minimal npm-workspace fixture under crates/seshat-scanner/tests/fixtures/js_monorepo/ so subsequent unit and e2e tests have a realistic input to exercise.
acceptanceCriteria: Create crates/seshat-scanner/tests/fixtures/js_monorepo/package.json with "workspaces": ["packages/*"]
Create packages/shared/package.json with "name": "@myorg/shared" and packages/shared/src/index.ts exporting a const Button
Create packages/web/package.json with "name": "@myorg/web" and packages/web/src/App.ts importing { Button } from "@myorg/shared"
Fixture files committed and discoverable by cargo test
cargo test --workspace passes (no regressions)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixture committed in 2026-05-17 ralphy run. Root package.json declares npm workspaces ['packages/*']; packages/shared exports const Button; packages/web imports it from '@myorg/shared'. cargo test --workspace + cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings both clean.

userStories.title: Implement extract_js_package_names() in manifest.rs
userStories.description: As the scanner, I need to extract workspace package names from a root package.json so internal imports can be resolved. Parses both the array form ("workspaces": ["packages/*"]) and the Yarn-classic object form ("workspaces": { "packages": [...] }), expands globs, reads each matched directory's package.json, and includes the root "name" when present.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fn extract_js_package_names(path: &Path, content: &str, manifest_dir: &Path) -> Vec<String> in crates/seshat-scanner/src/manifest.rs parallel to extract_crate_names()
Handles array form "workspaces": ["packages/*", "apps/*"]
Handles Yarn-classic object form "workspaces": { "packages": ["packages/*"], "nohoist": [...] }
Expands glob patterns via the glob crate; reads each matched package.json and collects its "name"
Includes root "name" if present (single-package projects)
Returns both scoped (@org/pkg) and unscoped (my-pkg) names verbatim — no normalisation that loses the @scope
Rename test analyze_manifests_package_json_has_empty_internal_names to ..._has_populated_internal_names and assert workspace names are returned
Add unit tests covering the 11 PRD §III test rows in manifest.rs::tests
Existing parse_package_json still extracts declared dependencies — analyze_manifests_end_to_end (manifest.rs:1206) unchanged
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented 2026-05-18. Added fn extract_js_package_names(path, content, manifest_dir) -> Vec<String> in crates/seshat-scanner/src/manifest.rs handling array form, Yarn-classic object form, and root "name". Glob expansion uses the new `glob = "0.3"` workspace dep (added to root Cargo.toml + seshat-scanner Cargo.toml). Names returned verbatim (no hyphen → underscore normalisation that would lose @scope). analyze_manifests now calls this for ManifestType::PackageJson with manifest_dir = path.parent(). Test analyze_manifests_package_json_has_empty_internal_names → analyze_manifests_package_json_has_populated_internal_names. 13 new unit tests cover PRD §III rows 1-5 plus array/object/literal/glob forms, scope preservation, missing package.json, missing name. cargo test --workspace + clippy + fmt all clean.

userStories.title: Implement parse_pnpm_workspace_yaml() for pnpm monorepos
userStories.description: As the scanner, I need to read pnpm-workspace.yaml's packages: field when root package.json lacks a "workspaces" entry, so pnpm-based monorepos are also detected.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fn parse_pnpm_workspace_yaml(path: &Path) -> Vec<String> in crates/seshat-scanner/src/manifest.rs
Parses the packages: list from pnpm-workspace.yaml using serde_yml (confirm crate is already in tree; add to Cargo.toml only if missing)
Expands globs and reads each matched package.json's "name" (reuse the helper from US-002)
Called only when root package.json has no "workspaces" field
New unit test with a fixture YAML covering packages: ["packages/*"]
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Union JS/TS names into orchestrator workspace_crates
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need to merge JS/TS workspace names into the same workspace_crates slot the Rust/Python pipelines already use, plus any local_packages config extras, so the resolver has a single unified internal-names list.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend the workspace_crates union+persist block at crates/seshat-scanner/src/orchestrator.rs:467 (around line 481) to include JS/TS names alongside Rust/Python ones
Union semantics match existing Rust/Python merge (no duplicates, no precedence regressions)
Persisted key remains "workspace_crates" (no rename — out of scope)
If FW-5 per-branch slot is present in tree, write to per-branch slot; otherwise global slot
New unit test reads workspace package.json files, asserts persisted JSON contains all expected workspace names plus any local_packages extras
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement resolve_js_internal_import() in dependencies resolver
userStories.description: As the graph dependencies resolver, I need to match JS/TS absolute imports against the internal-names list using longest-prefix-wins, so imports like @myorg/shared and @myorg/shared/utils both resolve to their workspace package while react stays external.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fn resolve_js_internal_import(module: &str, internal_names: &[String], suffix_index: &SuffixIndex) -> Option<PathBuf> in crates/seshat-graph/src/dependencies.rs
Sort internal_names by length-desc once at function top so @myorg/shared is checked before @myorg/sh
Exact-match case: @myorg/shared with internal @myorg/shared → resolve to package root (index.{ts,js} via suffix index)
Prefix-match case: @myorg/shared/utils with internal @myorg/shared → strip prefix, resolve "utils" via suffix index
Unscoped prefix-match: my-app/components with internal my-app → strip and resolve "components"
Negative case: "react" not in list → returns None (external)
Wire into resolve_import after the existing starts_with('.') short-circuit branch
Comparison is string-level before any module-segment splitting (handles @scope/name as a single token)
Add unit tests in crates/seshat-graph/src/dependencies.rs::tests covering all four Resolution Rules cases
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: End-to-end test: scan js_monorepo fixture and resolve cross-package import
userStories.description: As the scanner+graph pipeline, I need an end-to-end test that scans the js_monorepo fixture from US-001 and confirms query_dependencies on packages/shared returns packages/web as a dependent, proving the full extract → persist → resolve loop works.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an integration test that scans crates/seshat-scanner/tests/fixtures/js_monorepo/
Test asserts query_dependencies (or equivalent graph query) on a file in packages/shared returns the importer in packages/web as a dependent
Test exercises the full extract → orchestrator union → persist → resolver loop end-to-end (no mocks for the resolver path)
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
cargo fmt --check clean
Typecheck passes
